original_text:
### Required Elements Within Sections
- Clinical claims require footnote markers (†) with footnote text clearly separated and noticeably smaller (§1.2).
- Patient quotes: attributed as `- [Name], LISRAYA patient`, italic or distinguished styling, paired with warm lifestyle imagery.
- Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI all live in the **locked header/footer components — never reproduce in sections.**

quotes: Clinical claims require footnote markers (†) with footnote text clearly separated and noticeably smaller (§1.2).

rules.intent: Ensure clinical substantiation is traceable and distinguishable per regulatory footnote conventions.
rules.rule_text: Every clinical claim must carry a footnote marker (†) linking to footnote text; the footnote text must be visually and spatially separated from body copy and set noticeably smaller than surrounding text (e.g., footnote scale = 12px/18px vs. 16px/24px body).

quotes: italic or distinguished styling, paired with warm lifestyle imagery
Patient quotes: attributed as `- [Name], LISRAYA patient`, italic or distinguished styling, paired with warm lifestyle imagery.

rules.intent: Give patient testimonials a consistent, authentic, and legally traceable presentation.
rules.rule_text: Patient quotes must be attributed in the fixed format "- [Name], LISRAYA patient", rendered in italic or otherwise visually distinguished styling, and always paired with warm, realistic lifestyle photography.

effect.subject: Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI

quotes: locked header/footer components — never reproduce in sections
Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI all live in the **locked header/footer components — never reproduce in sections.**

rules.intent: Preserve regulatory and legal integrity by keeping mandated legal/compliance content confined to its single locked location.
rules.rule_text: The Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication statement, and ISI must live exclusively within the locked header/footer (Top Matter/End Matter) components and must never be reproduced elsewhere in body sections.
